items.q: Which component handles visual and spatial information?
items.why: Students mix up the slave systems. The phonological loop is for auditory/verbal information; the sketchpad is for visual/spatial. The central executive coordinates but doesn't store.

items.q: According to Maslow, when can self-actualisation be achieved?
items.correct: Only after lower deficiency needs are largely met.
items.misconception: Anyone can self-actualise at any time through willpower.
items.why: Students miss the hierarchical structure. You cannot pursue growth needs while physiological, safety, love, and esteem needs are unmet - that's the whole point of a hierarchy.

items.q: What does a correlation of -0.8 indicate?
items.misconception: A weak relationship (because it is negative).
items.why: Students treat the negative sign as 'weak'. The sign shows DIRECTION (as one variable goes up, the other goes down); the absolute value shows STRENGTH. -0.8 is strong.

items.q: Low serotonin is most often linked to:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Depression. “Schizophrenia” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
Correct answer: Adrenal cortex. “Pineal” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Year Kosfeld et al. published the oxytocin trust study:
items.why: Exact answer: 2005. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
